task_completion.description: Access the wger fitness manager login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger login page is visible in the web browser at http://localhost.
The browser shows a logged-in state for the wger application, typically with the username 'admin' or an admin dashboard visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the account settings or profile page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user profile, preferences, or settings page is displayed in the browser, showing account configuration options.

task_completion.description: Change the weight unit to pounds (lb)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The weight unit setting (dropdown, radio button, or similar input) is set to 'lb' or 'pounds' instead of 'kg' or 'kilograms'.
A confirmation message indicating the settings were successfully saved is visible, or the settings page is shown in a saved state with 'lb' or 'pounds' selected.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser interface, not terminal commands, curl requests, or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual wger application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows http://localhost and the wger interface is genuine, not a text editor or mock page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open or used to modify the page's HTML to falsely display 'lb'.
